Ad Text:
Ready to define the future of AI quality for a household name in Australian retail?

We are seeking an elite, hands-on Senior AI Test Engineer for a mission-critical contract. You will be deployed into the innovation hub of one of Australia's largest retail giants, taking full ownership of the integrity of their next-generation machine learning models.

What you'll be doing:

In this hands-on architectural role, you will be the technical engine for AI quality, moving beyond traditional testing to build and own the entire validation process by:

Architecting and implementing the overarching testing strategy, frameworks, and methodologies for complex AI and ML workloads from scratch.
Building out the core test automation infrastructure using your deep expertise in Playwright, C#, .NET, and Azure.
Overseeing the creation of the final regression test suite and implementing advanced validation techniques (like LLM-as-a-Judge) to gate production releases.
Acting as the primary release gatekeeper, analysing cross-functional reports to manage AI model drift and provide the final quality sign-off.
Evaluating existing manual regression tests and identifying prime candidates to be transitioned into sophisticated, automated AI scripts.
Initial 3 month contract with possible contract extensions - lots of exciting work here!
What You'll Bring:

Expert-level, hands-on proficiency architecting and building test automation frameworks, especially for AI/ML systems.
Deep expertise in Playwright, C#/.NET, and Azure is essential.
Proven experience acting as a quality gatekeeper for machine learning models, with a strong understanding of concepts like model drift.
A meticulous, architectural mindset—you thrive on building robust systems, not just executing scripts within them.
The ability to work autonomously and provide strategic guidance. You are a trusted expert known for taking ownership and getting things done.
Experience with titles such as MLOps Engineer, AI SDET, or Machine Learning Test Engineer is highly advantageous.
What else is in it for you?

Iconic Brand: A unique chance to architect a critical function for a household name.
Bleeding-Edge Work: This is a rare opportunity to work at the forefront of AI testing, implementing advanced concepts like Playwright MCP and LLM-as-a-Judge.
Premium Rates & Impact: Command a top-tier day rate for a high-visibility project where your contribution is central to success.
If you are a technical expert who prides yourself on building flawless, intelligent systems, we want to hear from you. Apply now for immediate consideration.

Job Title:  TECHNO FUNCTIONAL CONSULTANT L3

City:  Sydney

State/Province:  New South Wales

Wipro Limited (NYSE: WIT, BSE: 507685, NSE: WIPRO) is a leading technology services and consulting company focused on building innovative solutions that address clients’ most complex digital transformation needs. Leveraging our holistic portfolio of capabilities in consulting, design, engineering, and operations, we help clients realize their boldest ambitions and build future-ready, sustainable businesses. With over 230,000 employees and business partners across 65 countries, we deliver on the promise of helping our customers, colleagues, and communities thrive in an ever-changing world. For additional information, visit us at www.wipro.com.

Do

1. Bridging the gap between project and support teams through techno-functional expertise

For a new business implementation project, drive the end to end process from business requirement management to integration & configuration and production deployment
Check the feasibility of the new change requirements and provide optimal solution to the client with clear timelines
Provide techno-functional solution support for all the new business implementations while building the entire system from the scratch
Support the solutioning team from architectural design, coding, testing and implementation
Understand the functional design as well as technical design and architecture to be implemented on the ERP system
Customize, extend, modify, localize or integrate to the existing product by virtue of coding, testing & production
Implement the business processes, requirements and the underlying ERP technology to translate them into ERP solutions
Write code as per the developmental standards to decide upon the  implementation methodology
Provide product support and maintenance to the clients for a specific ERP solution and resolve the day to day queries/ technical problems which may arise
Create and deploy automation tools/ solutions to ensure process optimization and increase in efficiency
Sink between technical and functional requirements of the project and provide solutioning/ advise to the client or internal teams accordingly
Support on-site manager with the necessary details wrt any change and off-site support

Mandatory Skills: Adobe AEM . Experience: >10 YEARS . Reinvent your world. We are building a modern Wipro. We are an end-to-end digital transformation partner with the boldest ambitions. To realize them, we need people inspired by reinvention. Of yourself, your career, and your skills. We want to see the constant evolution of our business and our industry. It has always been in our DNA - as the world around us changes, so do we. Join a business powered by purpose and a place that empowers you to design your own reinvention.

Apply now

Senior Software Engineer (AI)

📍 Sydney, Australia
💰 Up to $210,000 package (inc Super) + Bonus
🕒 Permanent | Mid-level to Mid-Senior


About

Driven by our AI-first strategy, we are creating AI-native solutions and an AI-empowered workforce to deliver best-in-class experiences for over 2 million Australians. You will join our talented Enterprise AI Enablement team to build leading-edge solutions within our agentic AI ecosystem.

What Role Will You Play?

As a Software Engineer within the Enterprise AI Enablement team, you will be at the forefront of designing and building robust, scalable backend systems that power our AI-native solutions.

With a strong emphasis on software engineering excellence, you will spend the majority of your time writing production-grade code and automating complex workflows to create a resilient foundation for our agentic AI ecosystem.

You will:

Take end-to-end ownership of products in a modern DevOps culture
Design, architect, build, test, and operate scalable backend systems
Work alongside data scientists in a multidisciplinary AI team
Implement LLMOps best practices for deploying and monitoring AI models
Build reliable, observable, and production-ready AI platforms

This role is ideal for a software engineer who has transitioned into Data/ML & AI and wants to build enterprise-grade AI systems at scale.

What You Offer

5+ years' experience as a Software Engineer in an agile DevOps environment
Strong object-oriented programming experience (Python preferred)
Experience across the full SDLC: design, build, test, and support mission-critical production platforms
Proven ability to write robust, production-grade code and automate complex workflows
Hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines
Experience managing deployments in containerised cloud environments (Docker/Kubernetes)
Exposure to modern agentic AI frameworks and concepts such as:
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
MCP
GCP Vertex AI
Google ADK
LangChain / LangGraph
LlamaIndex
Knowledge of LLMOps best practices for model experimentation, deployment, and monitoring

If you feel you possess the relevant skills to the above, apply now with most updated CV!

Based in the College of Engineering and Information Technology, Adelaide City Campus East
Full-time, fixed term position until December 2026
Level A - $81,293 - $109,307 per annum (plus 17% superannuation)

We are seeking and experienced Research Engineer to work on AI driven decision making in the School of Computer Science and Information Technology.

In this role, you will work with our partners Praetorian Aeronautics and their testing team to field test emerging AI agent-based technology and push forward projects of national significance. You will develop state of the art AI-driven decision-making solutions, learn first-hand about the current state of integrating AI-driven decision-making solutions as they operate in varied scenarios and will have a meaningful impact on the future direction of critically deployed solutions.

Working with other researchers and engineers in the team, you will share broad goals in decision making in contested and dynamic environments, multi-agent reinforcement learning, planning and secure and robust machine learning solutions.

The ideal candidate will enjoy working in a team with a blue-sky research focus and with a hands-on approach to experimentation allied to strong theoretical underpinnings. A relevant first degree in computer science, engineering or related discipline is essential. You will be a strong team player and be able to communicate and work with the investigators and industrial partners.

At Adelaide University, we create the opportunities you need to achieve your ambitions – because when you thrive, we thrive.  

We are transforming education for contemporary learners and global citizens. Building on a proud legacy and shaped by bold ambition, it’s a place of excellence and equity, where our vibrant community of staff are united by our purpose to inspire Australia’s future change-makers and create a better tomorrow.  

Work that matters  

The University’s College of Engineering and Information Technology brings together expertise across aviation, computer science, construction management, and the full spectrum of 21st-century engineering disciplines including civil, mechanical, electrical, chemical, environmental, and biomedical engineering. With strengths in AI, energy transition technologies, advanced manufacturing, biomedical innovations, and sustainable infrastructure, research is seamlessly integrated into teaching. Students learn in first-class facilities alongside leading researchers ‘what’s discovered one day is taught the next’ equipping graduates to drive innovation and tackle the grand challenges of our time.

The team  

Our team think boldly, tackle complex challenges, and utilise data-driven insights to create solutions that strengthen communities. Our graduates are innovators and entrepreneurs, designing and building a future that is more sustainable, inclusive, and connected.

Visit the AU website to learn more about the College of Engineering and Information Technology.

Our people   

Our people are driven by purpose, intellectual curiosity, and a genuine commitment to lifelong learning. We value authenticity, foster innovation, and work collaboratively, drawing on diverse perspectives in our pursuit of excellence.

This role suits an individual who thrives in a collaborative, forward-thinking environment. You will work alongside a diverse community of colleagues, access state-of-the-art facilities, and contribute to a broad range of research activities—ensuring your work is both engaging, and impactful.

Learn more about our people, what we stand for and what we offer at Careers at AU.

Experience  

To join our community and thrive in this role, you will likely have the following skills and experience:  

Qualifications:

A PhD in the relevant discipline or have equivalent qualifications or research experience.

Experience:

Demonstrated ability to conduct independent research and development, including leading research, where applicable, in reinforcement learning, planning, optimisation and heuristics in Counter UAS, weapon target assignment problems or similar.
Strong track record of high-quality publications and conference presentations relative to opportunity
Evidence of being involved in professional activities related to the research
Demonstrated ability to guide and oversee postgraduate and honours students in the research area and supervise research-support staff
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to communicate across disciplines and collaborate with industry
High level organisational skills, including the ability to prioritise and manage workload within and busy environment and meet competing deadlines

Our commitment to inclusion and diversity  

We are committed to fostering a culture of inclusion where diversity is celebrated and everyone feels respected and valued. Adelaide University is an equal opportunity employer, committed to creating a safe, inclusive, and equitable workplace where everyone can thrive. We strongly encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability, and people of all ages, genders, cultural backgrounds, sexual orientations, and gender identities. We are committed to supporting flexible working arrangements and providing reasonable adjustments throughout the recruitment process.  

Launch your future with Adelaide University now.

Applying is simple. Simply click on the Apply Now button and upload:  

your current CV  
a cover letter that tells us why you’re excited about the role  

The online application form will list the specific selection criteria that you need to address.

Submit your application by 11:30pm, Tuesday 24 March 2026. 

The University reserves the right to close this advertisement before the closing date if a suitable candidate is identified.   

Please note that the role description is not attached to this advertisement as it is currently being finalised.     

For further information about this opportunity, please contact (quoting reference number 492563):   

Rachael Cossons

Talent Acquisition Adviser

+61 8 8302 1700| careers.adelaideuniversity@adelaide.edu.au

 

Appointment is subject to a satisfactory National Police Check (NPC) being obtained and maintained. 

Applications welcomed from Australian or NZ citizens, Australian permanent residents and those who have the legal right to work in Australia for the term of appointment.

Analytics Engineer – Asset Performance

Department: Operations – Asset Management & Utilities
Start Date: ASAP
Duration: 5-Month Contract
Roster: 5:2 (8 hours per day, 40 hours per week)
Location: Perth CBD

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Analytics Engineer – Asset Performance to support a leading mining operation in delivering high-quality performance and asset reporting solutions.
This role will focus on developing a comprehensive reporting suite for the Water Management team to automate monthly water balance reporting, along with performance, reliability, and continuous improvement reporting. The position will also support broader operational performance reporting across Rail and Heavy Mobile Equipment (HME).
Working within an engineering-led analytics team, you will leverage your expertise in Snowflake SQL and Power BI to design scalable data models and pipelines that enable advanced analytics and strategic decision-making. You will play a key role in building the analytical foundation that empowers engineering stakeholders to analyse, interpret, and act on operational data.
This role requires someone who can transform large, complex datasets into structured, reliable, and accessible information for reporting and visualisation. You will also collaborate closely with internal technology teams to ensure seamless data integration between front-end and back-end systems, delivering trusted insights across the business.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain Snowflake SQL data models and pipelines to support asset performance analytics and monthly water management reporting
Design and build Power BI dashboards and reports for water balance, reliability, and continuous improvement processes
Support the Asset Performance team with ongoing development and maintenance of operational reporting
Drive continuous improvement in data quality through investigation, validation, and proactive issue resolution
Utilise GitHub for version control, code review, and collaborative development
Maintain robust documentation and governance across analytical systems
About You

Strong experience working with SQL Server and/or Snowflake
Proven expertise in Power BI development
Experience working with delay accounting data highly regarded
Familiarity with Water Management, Rail, or mining operations strongly preferred
Experience with Python or similar data tools is advantageous
Highly motivated, analytical, and eager to expand knowledge in asset performance and operational analytics
Benefits

Perth CBD office location
Opportunity to work on large-scale mining operations
Exposure to complex asset performance and operational datasets
Collaborative, engineering-led analytics environment
Professional development and skill growth in advanced data modelling and analytics

AI Engineer - Conversational. Leading the day-to-day advancement of AI transformation at a fast-paced and leading adventure retailer.

Are you driven by innovation and thrive on building scalable, high-impact conversational AI solutions? At 4WD Supacentre, we don't just keep up with change — we drive it. As an AI Engineer – Conversational, you'll play a pivotal role in shaping how our customers interact with us, both online and over the phone. From intelligent website chat to AI-powered voice in our contact centre, you'll be hands-on in building the next generation of customer experience. Join us at our Sydney Olympic Park Office, NSW. This role calls for a genuine expert in conversational AI — someone who has spent the last 3+ years immersed in building and delivering real-world chat and voice solutions, and brings 10+ years of broader technology experience to back it up. This is a hands-on role sitting within a team of around 15 talented technology specialists. Given the calibre we're seeking, only candidates who closely meet or exceed these requirements will be contacted.

About the Role

As an AI Engineer – Conversational at 4WD Supacentre, you'll take ownership of designing, building and deploying AI-driven chat and voice solutions that transform the way our customers engage with us. This is a hands-on, technical role working within a fast-moving team that values delivery and innovation in equal measure. Reporting to the Technology & Project Director.

We are on an exciting journey to implement AI chat across our ecommerce website and AI voice within our contact centre environment. We currently operate Salesforce for both voice calls and website chat. We are open to best-of-breed conversational AI tooling to complement it. You'll evaluate, recommend and implement the right solutions, integrating them seamlessly into our existing technology landscape.

This is a rare opportunity to shape conversational AI capability from the ground up inside a growing Australian retail business.

Role Responsibilities

Design and implement AI-powered chat solutions for the 4WD Supacentre website, delivering intelligent, personalised customer interactions

Architect and deploy AI voice solutions within the contact centre environment, reducing handling time and improving customer experience

Work closing with our internal team on system integrations that can be leveraged by the conversational AI systems to automate and enrich requests and experiences with our customers

Evaluate and recommend conversational AI platforms and tooling suited to a retail and ecommerce environment

Integrate conversational AI solutions with Salesforce CRM and other existing business systems

Build, train and continuously improve NLP models, intents, entities and conversation flows

Define and monitor key performance metrics for chat and voice AI solutions (containment rate, CSAT, deflection, resolution)

Collaborate with contact centre, digital and ecommerce teams to align AI capabilities with business needs

Manage third-party vendor relationships for conversational AI platforms and voice providers

Document technical designs, integration architectures and operational runbooks

Stay current with advancements in generative AI and large language models (LLMs) and apply them where appropriate

Support QA and testing of conversation flows prior to go-live

Contribute to the broader AI transformation roadmap across the business

About you

You are a technically sharp and commercially aware AI engineer who gets genuine satisfaction from seeing a well-built conversation flow delight a customer. You have implemented AI conversational solutions in a large multi-store retailer in Australia, either via conversational chat on an ecommerce platform, and/or you have implemented conversation voice for a call centre. You are deeply skilled at AI conversational implementations, and have done it multiple times successfully at scale. You are a great team player, with this burning feeling inside to achieve more, to not settle for average. You are someone who never says, ‘it cannot be done’, but rather leave no stone unturned to overcome difficulties.

You'll Bring:

Proven hands-on experience designing and deploying conversational AI solutions — chatbots, virtual agents or AI voice

Deep knowledge of one or more conversational AI platforms such as Salesforce Einstein Bots, Amazon Lex, Nuance, Eleven Labs, Genesys AI, LivePerson, Cognigy, Kore.ai or similar

Experience integrating AI chat and voice solutions with CRM platforms, particularly Salesforce Service Cloud

Strong understanding of NLP/NLU concepts including intent classification, entity extraction and dialogue management

Experience with AI voice solutions and telephony platforms such as Genesys Cloud, Amazon Connect, Twilio, Eleven Labs or similar

Proficiency with APIs and integration patterns (REST, webhooks) to connect AI platforms with back-end systems

Familiarity with large language models (LLMs) and generative AI and how they can be applied within conversational experiences

Experience defining and tracking conversational AI KPIs and iterating on performance

Strong documentation skills — able to produce clear technical and functional specifications

Excellent communication skills with the ability to work across technical and non-technical stakeholders

Required Skills and Qualifications:

3+ years experience working in conversational AI products and solutions (non negotiable)

Demonstrated end-to-end delivery of AI chat or voice conversational solutions in a commercial environment

5+ years working in a retailer / ecommerce environment in Australia

5+ years in a technology engineering or AI/ML role

Experience with contact centre platforms such as Amazon Connect, Salesforce Voice or Twilio

Experience working in a retail or ecommerce business in Australia

Strong proficiency with cloud platforms — AWS, Google Cloud or Azure

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or a related field

Strong proficiency in Agile, Scrum or hybrid delivery methodologies

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or Information Technology

Beneficial Skills and Qualifications (but not essential):

Hands-on experience with Salesforce Service Cloud

Experience with integration platforms such as Workato

Experience working in a business with their own distribution centres / warehouses

Experience with SAP, Salesforce, UKG, Magento, Shopify

Agile or Scrum Master certification

Experience working with third-party vendors and managing external delivery partners

What’s in it for you?

Competitive remuneration package

Exclusive employee discounts on the Adventure Kings product range

5 days per week at our Sydney Olympic Park Office

Parking provided

Be part of a dynamic, rapidly evolving, fast-growing company

If you want to be a part of our future, have the opportunity to grow then apply NOW!

*Please note: Only applicants with full, unrestricted Australian working rights will be considered for this role. We are not offering visa sponsorship at this time. Recruitment agencies and third-party inquiries will not be accepted.

Hybrid | Full-time

Build software that helps organisations do good - simply.

Red Arc builds supporter engagement software for nonprofits. Our tools are used by organisations including Blackdog, ActionAid and Habitat for Humanity to work smarter, connect better with supporters, and increase their impact.

We’re now building the next generation of our Supporter Engagement Platform - including AI-powered automation agents - and are looking for a versatile, product-minded Software Engineer to join our small, experienced team.

This is not a narrow feature-delivery role.

We’re looking for someone who enjoys variety: building new capabilities, improving existing systems, helping customers when things don’t go as planned, and stepping up when needed.

If you enjoy owning problems end-to-end, from idea to shipped product, from bug report to fix - you’ll feel very at home here.

About Red Arc

Red Arc is a Sydney-based software company building supporter engagement tools for nonprofits worldwide.

We extend Blackbaud’s Raiser’s Edge NXT ecosystem through:

Letter Box – donor acknowledgements and communications

Marketing Connector – email and marketing integrations

Engagement Tile – supporter insights

Supporter Engagement Platform – including AI Automation Agents

We’re a small, experienced team - global by choice, values-led by design - focused on building software that is simple, beautiful and genuinely helpful.

Working with NonProfits

Nonprofits operate with a distinct culture that differs significantly from commercial environments.

Fundraising and supporter engagement involve specialised concepts, language and workflows that aren’t always intuitive. Solutions that are technically sound can miss the mark without an understanding of how fundraisers actually work day to day.

We’re looking for someone who values NonProfit understanding as much as beautiful architecture - and who enjoys learning the nuances of a sector built around trust, relationships and long-term impact.

Experience in the nonprofit sector is a strong advantage, but curiosity, humility and empathy matter just as much.

The Role

This is a full-stack engineering role covering two core areas:

Product engineering

Design, build and evolve our platform and applications

Work closely with product, engineering and customers

Deliver reliable, maintainable systems

Support engineering

Investigate and fix production issues

Communicate directly with customers when problems arise

Improve reliability, performance and observability

Provide engineering-level support when required

This is not a call-centre support role - but it is real ownership of production systems used daily by nonprofits.

As part of a small team, you’ll also help keep things moving when needed, including deployments, fixes and customer support continuity.

AI is core to this role

AI is not a side project at Red Arc.

We:

Build AI automation agents into our products

Use AI heavily in our development workflow

Design systems explicitly to be usable as tools for agents

You don’t need to be an ML researcher - but you do need to be curious, practical and comfortable working with AI-enabled software.

Experience

Required Experience

2+ years professional software development experience

Backend experience (JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Java, C# or similar)

Experience with backend frameworks (Node.js, .NET, Django, Spring, etc.)

Experience with a modern frontend framework (Angular, React or Vue)

Experience building and/or using REST API's

Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure)

Experience with modern software development practices e.g. Git, Code Review, CI/CD pipelines

Experience writing and maintaining automated tests

Desirable experience

AWS (Lambda, DynamoDB, API Gateway, CloudFormation)

TypeScript / Node.js

Angular

SQL and NoSQL databases

DevOps or operational experience

CRM or third-party system integrations

Practical experience with AI-assisted development or agent-based systems

What we value

Strong customer empathy

Ownership mentality

Clear communication

Practical problem-solving

Curiosity and continuous learning

Comfort working in a small startup environment

Core Values

At Red Arc our core values are what makes us who we are, informing all our decisions from the smallest to the largest.

Be nice

Think different

We care for our people

Integrity is everything

Have faith

Be nimble

(and) have great stickers

Apply

When applying, please include a short note covering:

Why this role appeals to you

Your experience across product engineering and support

Your interest in AI-powered software

Your favourite nonprofit - and why

About Us 

Future Secure AI (FSAI) is a leading enterprise AI company that was created in deep partnership with one of the world's largest financial institutions.

Operating as an enterprise company with deep relationships at C-suite with large global enterprises, FSAI believes humans and AI Workers will collaborate seamlessly to raise organisational performance. FSAI has dozens of partnerships with leading companies in most industries and regions globally.

FSAI has a strategic focus on expanding globally as we continue our extraordinary journey to transform the world with the most cutting-edge artificial intelligence.

The Opportunity

We are hiring Senior AI Engineers (Software) to help us build the product and platform. In this role, you’ll lead customer-facing engagements, collaborating closely with customer and partner technical teams, alongside our internal squad to enhance tech solutions. 

This role requires you to efficiently navigate between detailed coding and broader business and product strategies. You must be highly organised, adept in cross-functional teams, and passionate about advancing AI technology.

Key responsibilities

Enable enterprise customers to deploy a diverse and dynamic range of AI agents from multiple third-party platforms (including models, LLMs, micro-services and even orchestration layers) through their own secure and scalable platform.

Develop innovative AI products for reputable global clients, focusing on Node.js, and integrating AI with use of LangChain, LangGraph, Flowise

Discover and incorporate new partnerships, products, and technologies, including cutting-edge LLMs and AI models, from both established and emerging vendors.

Design and implement algorithms specifically for AI agents, utilizing your Node.js expertise and understanding of AI frameworks.

Strategize on the seamless integration of new features and APIs across various platforms in large-scale enterprise environments, leveraging your Node.js and potential Python skills.

Exhibit a deep commitment to understanding user needs and meticulous attention to detail, ensuring our solutions not only meet but exceed user expectations.

Uphold and exceed enterprise-grade compliance standards, such as SOC2, ensuring our products are secure and reliable.

Requirements

Ideally you bring at least 7 years of software development experience; preferably with knowledge of AI concepts including natural language processing, machine learning, and neural networks. 

Not only do you have impressive problem-solving skills, we also value your leadership mindset and ability to proactively take charge in a fast moving environment. 

To be successful you must demonstrate the following:

Strong background in Node.js development is a must. Python is a plus.

Strong background in LangChain, AI Agent frameworks, and ability to apply these advanced AI solutions, OR you’re passionate about learning this (we will train you!)

Strong experience in building secure, scalable APIs.

Strong understanding of cloud environments (Azure, AWS, GCP)

Impressive problem solving / analytical skills

Impeccable attention to detail and integrity in your work ethic, you follow-through on doing what you said you’ll do.

Bonus

Experience with orchestration applications like Flowise 

Proficiency in CICD, Kubernetes and containerization technologies

If you're passionate about working with cutting-edge technology, collaborating with world-class leadership, and joining some of the best talent in the industry, we’d love to hear from you!

For a quicker response, we encourage you to apply directly through our website at https://www.futuresecure.ai/. There, you can watch a video about AI workers and explore the career opportunities we have available.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Join one of Australia's leading national retailers as an AI/ML Platform Engineering Manager, where you will play a pivotal role in shaping and scaling the organisation's next-generation machine learning capabilities. This is a permanent leadership opportunity overseeing a high-performing team of six engineers and driving end-to-end ownership of the AI/ML platform.

About the Role

In this role, you will lead the design, development, and delivery of critical AI/ML platform components supporting enterprise-wide machine learning initiatives. You will partner closely with data scientists, ML engineers, software developers, and platform teams to translate business needs into scalable and secure platform capabilities.

Key Responsibilities


Lead the architecture and development of AI/ML platform components including model training environments, feature stores, model registries, deployment pipelines, and monitoring frameworks.
Manage end-to-end delivery of platform enhancements, ensuring scalability, reliability, and high performance across all ML workloads.
Collaborate cross-functionally with technical and non-technical stakeholders to gather requirements and enable seamless delivery of AI/ML solutions.
Implement and champion best practices in MLOps, including model versioning, lifecycle management, CI/CD, observability, and platform security.
Drive continuous improvement by integrating new tools, frameworks, and automation to streamline and accelerate ML workflows.
Oversee platform reliability, incident response, operational support, and capacity planning to maintain enterprise-grade availability.
Ensure all platform capabilities adhere to organisational data governance, security standards, and relevant regulatory requirements.
Provide regular reporting, insights, and strategic recommendations to senior leadership on platform performance, risks, and future opportunities.


About You

You are a technical leader with a passion for building robust, scalable AI/ML platforms and empowering teams to deliver impactful machine learning outcomes. You thrive in a fast-paced, collaborative environment and bring deep experience in MLOps, automation, and cloud-native engineering.

What's on Offer


Permanent leadership role in a high-growth AI/ML environment
Opportunity to shape a major retailer's enterprise ML ecosystem
Lead and develop a team of six talented engineers
Competitive salary, benefits, and long-term career pathways

For more information contact Melissa Haddad at melissa.haddad@talentinternational.com

About The Company

TMGM is one of the fastest-growing CFD and Forex brokers in the Asia-Pacific region, trusted by over 150,000 clients globally. With a strong focus on innovation, client satisfaction, and regulatory compliance, we continue to expand our presence across international markets.

About The Role

As a Senior Data Engineer, you will be a technical leader responsible for the architecture, scalability, and reliability of our high-throughput, real-time data ecosystem. You will oversee the evolution of our data infrastructure, leveraging Kafka, RabbitMQ, Airflow, and ClickHouse to power mission-critical financial analytics. Your role is to bridge the gap between complex business requirements and high-performance engineering, ensuring our data pipelines can handle the rigours of real-time financial data processing.

Requirements

Lead the design and evolution of highly scalable, fault-tolerant ETL/ELT pipelines.

When you join Trend, you become part of a unique and diverse global family and you get to work towards a world safe for exchanging digital information.

ABOUT TRENDAI™

TrendAI™, a global cybersecurity leader, helps make the world safe for exchanging digital information. Fueled by decades of security expertise, global threat research, and continuous innovation, TrendAI™ cybersecurity platform protects hundreds of thousands of organizations and millions of individuals across clouds, networks, devices, and endpoints. As a leader in cloud and enterprise cybersecurity, the platform delivers a powerful range of advanced threat defense techniques optimized for environments like AWS, Microsoft, and Google, and central visibility for better, faster detection and response. With 7,000 employees across 65 countries, TrendAI™ enables organizations to simplify and secure their connected world.

POSITION OVERVIEW 

TrendAI™ is seeking a Senior Solutions Engineer who is passionate about cybersecurity and AI-powered platforms, thrives in direct customer engagement, and excels at helping organisations solve complex challenges. As a trusted technical advisor, you will influence outcomes across the entire customer lifecycle by retaining, expanding, and acquiring business through expert guidance, compelling demos, and strategic consultation. You will serve as the bridge between customer needs and platform capabilities, powering long-term value, adoption, and advocacy. If you are curious and love the challenge of providing the best security technology advice – then this is the job for you.

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES 

Serve as the primary technical advisor for enterprise customers and prospects, guiding them through the evaluation, adoption, and expansion of our cybersecurity platform. 
Develop a deep understanding of customer goals, challenges, and security requirements to align platform capabilities with meaningful outcomes. 
Lead impactful demos, presentations, and technical storytelling that clearly articulate value and build confidence across technical and executive audiences. 
Build and maintain trusted relationships with strategic partners to accelerate revenue and broaden market reach. 
Represent the company as a thought leader at industry events, panels, webinars, and field engagements. 
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Engineering, Marketing, Channel, and Customer Success to ensure aligned execution and exceptional customer experience. 
Provide product feedback and innovation insights to Product Management based on customer needs, technical gaps, and emerging market trends. 

SKILLS & EXPERIENCE 

Minimum of 5 years hands-on experience in Solutions Engineering, Sales Engineering, or Technical Pre-Sales roles.  
Proven experience working with technical decision-makers and executives in enterprise environments. 
Strong understanding of public cloud technologies (AWS, Azure, GCP). 
Broad technical acumen across cybersecurity, cloud architecture, networking, AI, risk visibility, and IT modernization. 
Experience designing/architecting solutions in on-premises, hybrid, and cloud-native environments. 
Ability to rapidly learn modern technologies and thrive in a fast-evolving cybersecurity landscape. 
Excellent communication and presentation skills with the ability to present to a variety of external audiences, including C-level executives. 
Proven experience and passion for public speaking, live demos, and customer education. 

DESIRABLE EXPERIENCE / CERTIFICATIONS

CISSP, CEH, CCSP, Security+, CySA+, CISM, CISA, or other ISACA certifications. 
Hands-on experience with API integrations, scripting, automation, or lightweight coding (Python, Bash, PowerShell). 
AI technology experience, such as applying AI/ML to cybersecurity workflows, leveraging LLMs or agentic AI for automation, or working with AI-driven detection and analytics systems. 

Please note: successful candidates will be required to undergo a background check and a national police check.

BENEFITS OF BEING A TRENDER!

We take employee wellbeing seriously. At TrendAI™, you will have access to Medical Insurance, Mental Health Support through access to EAP, Gym reimbursements, Paid Parental Leave etc.

We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We believe that our people are our greatest asset and we understand the importance of bringing diverse perspectives, approaches and experiences to our team. We are committed to building and supporting an inclusive workforce that truly reflects our global business and encourages people from all backgrounds to apply.

We’re proud to be recognised as a Great Place to Work®, a testament to our commitment to fostering a positive, inclusive, and empowering workplace culture.

We embrace change, empower people, and encourage innovation. Join Trend Micro and Thrive with us.

About Us

We are experts in board, CEO and C-Suite leadership with over 30 years of experience setting the gold standard in top-tier leadership development. Now, we’re looking to help leaders of all levels by automating, scaling, and augmenting our existing assessment and coaching processes. We move fast but we don't compromise quality. We need an engineer who can do both.

The Role

This is a founding engineering position. You will own the technical implementation of our product: building and maintaining the full stack, managing AI-assisted development workflows, and setting the engineering standards that the rest of the team will grow into.

You will not be doing this alone. A product-requirements document and MVP plan will be delivered to you by a senior technical consultant before you begin. Your job is to take this plan and execute it at high quality, with modern tooling, and with enough rigor that junior developers can contribute safely within the same system.

This role is for someone who has done the big-company thing and knows exactly what they'd do differently if given the chance.

What You'll Own

The full stack. Python backend, APIs, and user-facing applications. It will need to be architected cleanly and built to last.

AI-assisted development. You'll use agentic coding tools (Claude Code, Vibe, or equivalent) not as a novelty, but as a core part of how you work. We expect you to be fluent in this and to help define how our team uses these tools responsibly.

Code quality and standards. You'll establish the review processes, testing practices, and documentation norms that keep the codebase maintainable as the team grows.

Infrastructure and delivery. Cloud infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment, all owned end to end.

Junior developer oversight. As we grow, you'll bring on and guide junior contributors. The systems and standards you build now need to support that.

Who You Are

You have significant experience building and shipping production-grade systems — not just features, but entire stacks.

You're genuinely fluent with AI-assisted development. You've used LLM-based coding tools in your workflow and you have opinions about how to do it well.

You're comfortable with Python and cloud infrastructure. You write clean, testable, well-documented code as a matter of professional habit, not obligation.

You understand how to work from a specification. You don't need to invent the product, but you need to build it excellently.

You know how to move fast without creating chaos. You've lived through what happens when speed outpaces quality, and you've learned from it.

You're drawn to hard, meaningful problems. AI applied to human development and organizational performance is genuinely novel territory, and that excites you.




Compensation & Benefits

Option A — Market Rate (No Equity)

Competitive market-rate salary commensurate with experience

Full benefits package

Sydney, NSW in-person role with a team that respects your time and expertise

Option B — Below Market Rate + Equity

Salary below market rate, offset by meaningful equity in a company at an early, high-leverage stage

Full benefits package

Founding team-level ownership stake with real upside as we scale.

How to Apply

Send us your resume, a GitHub profile or project you're proud of, and short answers to the following:

Describe a time you balanced speed and quality under real pressure. What did you protect, and what did you let slide?

Tell us about a meaningful experience using AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor, or similar) to write or manage code. What worked, what didn't, and what would you do differently?

How would you approach building the MVP of an AI-powered executive coaching platform? Where would you start, and what would you be most careful about?

Work with cutting edge AI technology, making the world a safer and more secure place. DroneShield (ASX:DRO) offers an opportunity to solve some of world’s most challenging technical problems in the rapidly growing counter-drone sector.   

Our customers operate in some of the most challenging and high-stakes environments in the world, including military organisations, government agencies, airports, critical infrastructure operators, and law enforcement. Protecting airspace in these settings requires technology that performs under pressure and teams that understand what’s at stake. At DroneShield, employees work at the leading edge of counter-drone innovation, helping to address real-world security challenges as drone threats continue to evolve globally. 

With one of the largest listed defence company market capitalisations in Australia, now part of the ASX200 index, DroneShield is experiencing a period of hypergrowth. Revenue has surged from A$57 million in 2024 to over A$190 million in 2025, representing growth of more than 400% year-on-year, with record profitability and cashflow. The total addressable global market for counter-drone is assessed at approximately $100 billion, and is currently at the nascent stage with much of the growth still to come, with DroneShield well positioned as a global market leader, and the only public listed pure-play business in this sector.   

The company has grown from 11 employees in 2017 to over 450 staff globally today, and is on track to reach around 550 by the end of 2026. This expansion includes investment of over A$50 million annually in R&D, a global pipeline exceeding A$2.5 billion, and continuous scaling of production capacity to meet accelerating demand.  







About the role

DroneShield is seeking an Algorithms Software Engineer with relevant experience to join the team in Adelaide, South Australia. The position will report to the Director of Engineering, Sensors and Effectors.

The Sensors and Effectors team at Droneshield is focused on providing fully operational and qualified modular devices to enable new product development of high-end RF based sensors and effectors. These modules are re-used across various product configurations including man-portable, vehicle mounted and fixed site. Key technical challenges for the electronics aspect of these modules include the design of complex wide-band RF circuitry, high speed digital interface and high-power radio frequency output. DroneShield designs and builds the full signal chain from signal generation to antenna.

The primary focus of the role is to develop and maintain DroneShield’s RFAI Detection Capability, working closely with ML/AI, Data Science, RF and infrastructure teams to innovate and enhance DroneShield’s world leading Anti-Drone technology.

Responsibilities, Duties and Expectations 

Take drone detection algorithms from design to production using C++, working closely with a team of Algorithm and Software Engineers.
Develop and maintain simulation software used to assist algorithm development.
Design and implement data analysis and algorithm evaluation pipelines.
Conduct unit testing to ensure software quality and reliability.
Develop and maintain CI/CD pipelines.
Assist software & hardware development teams to make informed decisions about the future direction of complex deployed system design.
Advance the development of software architecture design processes within the team. Ensure software is designed for reliability, and performance on resource-constrained targets.

Qualifications, Experience and Skills 

Bachelor’s (or higher) degree in Computer Science, Electronics Engineering, a similar technical field of study, or equivalent practical experience.
3+ years of software development experience (preferred: Python, C++).
Ability to work in a multidisciplinary team, including communicating effectively with engineers from non-software / data science backgrounds.
Ability to turn complex algorithms and mathematical models into high-performance software, operating on sensor data.
Working knowledge of modern architectural patterns and software design patterns.
Experience with the following:
At least one compiled language (Go, C++, or C).
At least one scripting language (Python, JavaScript, or Shell).
Digital Signal Processing concepts and techniques.
Embedded Linux platforms.
Test Driven Development.
Revision Control.
Continuous Integration.

Who you are

You are comfortable deploying state of the art algorithmic models to deliver commercial value.
You are a lifelong learner. You’re self-taught and continuously learning. You are interested in keeping up with current best practices in your areas of expertise.
You are experienced working with modern infrastructure and tools.
You are comfortable running your software in Linux environments.
An abundance of ambition and motivation to grow the company and compete with the big players in the industry.

If you live in Australia or New Zealand, you've likely heard of AMP. But at a time when society is changing, we are too. We're now a nimbler business with new leadership and thinking.

For us, these are exciting times. There's a real potential for big thinkers to help us redefine what financial services could be. And turn our legacy into something even more positive and powerful for the future.

Help people create their tomorrow, while you create yours

We help people with their banking, super, retirement and finances. Through upturns, downturns, recessions, and major life transitions. Every day, we help people see and make more of their financial potential, so that they can create their tomorrow. And we've been doing it for over 170 years.

If we do our job well, we genuinely add to the prosperity of our country and its people.

How you'll make an impact

In this role, you'll be at the centre of how enterprise AI is designed, governed, and brought to life at scale. You'll engineer and operate the organisation's AI platforms across Microsoft Power Platform, Copilot Studio and Azure AI Foundry, ensuring they are secure, reliable, and ready for broad adoption. Your work will enable teams across the business to safely build and deploy AI agents and intelligent workflows, while maintaining strong guardrails around security, compliance, and Responsible AI.

You'll have a tangible impact on how AI moves from experimentation to trusted, production‑ready capability. By embedding robust CI/CD, governance, observability, and operational practices, you'll help ensure AI solutions are resilient, scalable, and sustainable. Along the way, you'll partner closely with architects, security, risk, and compliance teams, and mentor other engineers , helping uplift capability while avoiding key‑person dependency and strengthening the organisation's AI foundations for the long term.

What you'll bring to the role

You'll bring deep, hands‑on experience engineering and running enterprise platforms, ideally within Microsoft's AI and low‑code ecosystem or a comparable cloud environment. You're comfortable designing and developing AI agents and agentic workflows, and you understand what it takes to move from proof‑of‑concept to production including governance, lifecycle management, and operational reliability.

You have strong practical experience with CI/CD pipelines, environment management and release automation, alongside a solid grounding in cloud security, identity, access control, and secrets management. You're confident working with large language models, prompt design, grounding techniques, and evaluation approaches, and you bring a thoughtful, responsible mindset to AI delivery. Just as importantly, you're a collaborative engineer who can influence without authority, work closely with architects and stakeholders, and provide guidance and mentoring to lift the capability of those around you.

You'll thrive here if…

If you can adapt from BAU to the ambiguous with ease, you'll do well here. Change is never easy, so bring your commitment, grit and growth mindset.

Because we run lean, you'll be expected to jump in and deliver across a variety of areas. Meaning, you'll be closer to the action and executive decisions that influence where we go next.

If you're someone that can hold their own, you'll find AMP quite liberating.

Why we think you'll love working at AMP

Doing what we've always done is not an option, so your clever ideas will get airtime here. You'll be encouraged to speak up and try new things. If they don't work, we move on - better for it.

We know there's no one way of doing things. So, you won't have to sacrifice who you are or how you work to fit in here. We're inclusive and flexible in many of the ways you'd expect. And in some of the ways you wouldn't. As long as your health and wellbeing come first - at home and at work.

In fact, most of what makes AMP such a welcoming, enjoyable place to work are our people. Wherever you go, you'll find moments to connect, feel valued and do meaningful work.

Whether it's through our first-class leaders who are invested in you and your success. Through year-round opportunities to volunteer, fundraise and give back to the community. Or in the everyday challenges you face as we work together to strengthen this great organisation. Challenges that will stretch you, amplify your potential and compound the impact you have.

We believe in the power of inclusion and diversity

We're dedicated to fostering inclusion, diversity, and a warm feeling of belonging at AMP. It sparks creativity, ignites innovation, and turns up the dial on the quality of our decisions and performance. This not only makes our workplace more engaged, but also leads to better connections with our customers.

We're your allies in the search for the perfect fit - when you apply, let us know how we can support you to put your best self forward during our selection process.

We're also committed to enhancing employment opportunities for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, so we enthusiastically encourage candidates from these backgrounds to apply and explore our Reconciliation Plan on our website

Data Engineer | ASAP Start | Large Utilities Organisation

6 month contract with likely extensions
Up to $1000 p/d inc super 
Melbourne Location (Hybrid)
Primary Purpose of the Role:

Deliver data pipelines and platform enhancements across Databricks and AWS. Support the build of new data products using medallion architecture, data mesh patterns and solid engineering practices.

Key Accountabilities:

Build and enhance data pipelines in Databricks and AWS
Develop common data engineering components using Python
Apply integration patterns and reusable data engineering practices
Work with medallion architecture and data mesh design principles
Support data definition and modelling activities
Ensure quality, reliability and performance across all pipelines
Collaborate with platform, architecture and analytics teams to deliver outcomes
Provide technical input into design, testing and release processes
Essential Requirements:

Strong hands on experience in Databricks
Solid AWS infrastructure knowledge
Proficient in Python for data engineering
Experience building new pipelines and integration patterns
Understanding of medallion architecture and data mesh concepts
Strong grasp of data modelling and data definition
Ability to work in a fast paced environment with minimal supervision
If you have relevant experience, please submit your latest CV and we will be in touch shortly!

About Us

LGT is the largest Private Banking and Asset Management group in the world to be owned by an entrepreneurial family. As the family office of the Princely House of Liechtenstein, we have years of experience in the management of sizeable sums of assets.

With a shared passion for building an uncompromised business – we created something new from the ground up. A chance to leave behind the things that weren’t working, while enhancing the things that were. Our authentic and personalised client-first commitment. Our entrepreneurial spirit. Our focus in best-in-class advice. And our intimate understanding of the Australian private wealth landscape.

About the Opportunity

We are looking for a Data Engineer to join our Platform Engineering team on a 12‑month fixed‑term contract. This role bridges data engineering, analytics, and applied data science, with a strong focus on reusability, and regulatory alignment.
 

Key Responsibilities:

Contribute to the design and evolution of the enterprise data foundation, including core and presentation data layers, in line with the modern data stack and logical data models already defined.

Build well‑defined, reusable data products (datasets, features, semantic models) that can be consumed by analytics, Artificial Intelligence (AI) models, and downstream applications.

Partner with data engineers to define data structures that support historical accuracy, auditability, and lineage, which are critical in a regulated wealth environment.

Perform exploratory data analysis to identify patterns, data quality issues, and AI opportunities.

Support AI use cases by ensuring data is fit‑for‑purpose, well‑documented, and reproducible.

Work within the enterprise data governance framework, including Data quality rules and monitoring; Metadata, lineage, and glossary contributions.

Collaborate with Data Owners to resolve data issues and clarify definitions.

Ensure datasets and models meet regulatory, privacy, and audit requirements relevant to financial services.

Collaborate closely with engineering, architecture, compliance, and business teams.

Contribute to standards, templates, and best practices for analytics and data science delivery.

Support enablement of other teams by creating reusable assets and documentation.

Working alongside your IT infrastructure, project delivery and security colleagues, you will take ownership for the day to day operability of the data integration platform to ensure business functions run smoothly.

About You:

Skills & Experience

Strong experience with Python for data analysis and modelling (e.g. pandas, NumPy, scikit‑learn or equivalent).

Solid SQL skills and experience working with cloud data warehouses and lakehouses.

Experience working in a modern data platform (e.g. Microsoft Fabric, Synapse, Snowflake, Databricks).

Understanding of data modelling concepts (e.g. dimensional models, Data Vault or similar enterprise patterns).

Proven experience working on data foundations, not just dashboards or isolated models.

Experience creating reusable, governed datasets or features intended for multiple downstream consumers.

Familiarity with metadata management, data lineage, and data quality frameworks.

Experience supporting AI use cases or early‑stage production models is highly desirable.

Experience working in financial services or another regulated industry.

Awareness of data privacy, auditability, and model risk considerations.

Ability to balance innovation with control, documentation, and traceability.

Strong analytical thinking with the ability to explain complex concepts simply.

Comfortable working with ambiguity and helping shape the right data approach rather than being handed fully‑formed requirements.

Collaborative mindset and ability to work closely with senior engineers, architects, and business stakeholders.

Understanding of CI/CD pipeline experience.

Desirable Skills

Experience with Microsoft Purview or similar governance/cataloguing tools.

Exposure to GenAI use cases (LLMs, embeddings, retrieval‑augmented generation).

Experience contributing to an AI or Data Centre of Excellence model.

Experience in preparing data assets specifically for AI and GenAI use cases, including feature engineering, embedding, and structured/unstructured data preparation.

Degree or qualification in machine learning.

Role Competencies

Strong problem-solving abilities, with a logical and methodical approach to tasks.

Excellent communication skills, able to translate technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders.

Commitment to maintaining high-quality standards in all tasks.

Ability to manage changing priorities and work in a dynamic, and a proactive manner.

A passion for emerging technologies and an interest in industry developments in this fast-moving sector.

Qualifications

Industry certification in the above technologies will be highly desired.

Tertiary degree in software engineering, computer science or related principles.

Who we are:  
Master Builders Victoria (MBV) is the peak industry body representing Victoria’s building and construction sector. We support more than 6,000 members across domestic and commercial construction, including builders of all sizes, sub-contractors, suppliers and manufacturers. 
At MBV, you’ll be part of an inclusive, values-driven organisation that champions the industry and invests in its people. We provide a supportive environment where you can grow your career, develop new skills and make a genuine impact on an industry that shapes communities across Victoria. 
  
About the Role  
As the LMS Specialist, you’ll lead the design, development, and continuous improvement of our Learning Management System (LMS), delivering engaging and high quality training experiences that support our learners and the wider industry. 
You will be responsible for: 

Designing, maintaining and improving course frontend web architecture. 
Creating learning programs that build capability through online learning solutions, including coordinating individual learning components. 
Using markup languages like HTML to develop user friendly courses and web pages within Canvas. 
Exploring and, where possible, implementing API integrations between DVS, VET, CRM and Canvas systems. 

Skills, Attributes and Experience:  

Proven experience as a Learning Designer, LMS Specialist, or similar role (minimum 2+ years of elearning development). 
Experience using VETtrak, with the ability to navigate and utilise this Student Management System effectively. 
Proficiency in frontend and backend programming languages such as HTML, XML, JavaScript, Python, C++ or C#. 
Understanding of ASQA regulatory and compliance standards, particularly those relating to training and assessment (RTO Standards). 

What MBV can offer you:  

Hybrid work model, compressed working weeks, purchased annual leave 
Strong people engagement and values-based culture 
Paid parental leave, corporate partnerships, company wide lunches, events and activities

Senior Data Scientist – Customer & Retention
Location: Sydney
Salary: $190k–$200k base + super
Sector: Financial Services

The Opportunity This is a brand-new, high-impact role within a major financial services organisation where data science is measured by one thing: commercial impact. The focus is on building production-grade customer and retention models that directly influence revenue, growth, and portfolio performance.

This is not reporting or support analytics. It’s end-to-end ML ownership. You’ll design, build, deploy, monitor, and evolve models that drive customer retention, engagement, and lifetime value, with real influence across marketing, pricing, credit, collections, and customer strategy.

The role is heavily hands-on and technical. Most of your time will be spent on model development, data wrangling, feature engineering, hyperparameter optimisation, deployment, and monitoring, with some exposure to data engineering and MLOps.

What You’ll Be Doing
Build customer and retention models end-to-end
Lead ML solution design from business problem to production
Develop models in Python for batch and real-time deployment into Azure
Own the full model lifecycle: build, deploy, monitor, retrain
Partner with business and product teams to embed models into decisioning
Deliver models that directly impact revenue and P+L
Tech Environment
Data: Snowflake
Modelling: Python
Deployment: Azure ML Studio / Snowflake
Monitoring: Streamlit
Version Control: Azure DevOps
GenAI: Azure AI Foundry
What They’re Looking For
Strong experience building production ML models
Background in customer analytics, retention, or marketing modelling
Excellent Python and ML engineering capability
Experience with model deployment and monitoring
FS, lending, or mortgage exposure is a bonus, not required
